name: red-notice
description: Bill Browder's true story of investing in Russia, battling corruption, losing his lawyer Sergei Magnitsky to state murder, and creating the Magnitsky Act to sanction human rights abusers.
useCase: Navigating corrupt regimes, shareholder activism against crony capitalism, exposing state-sponsored crime, pursuing justice after murder, building international human rights campaigns.
knownRisks: Browder's perspective is from one side of an extremely polarized conflict with the Russian government
Russia has denied all allegations regarding the Magnitsky case and has charged Browder in absentia with fraud
The Magnitsky Act has been criticized by some as a tool of geopolitical pressure rather than pure human rights advocacy
This is a memoir — events are presented from Browder's personal perspective
